sanskrit:
दैवमेवापरे यज्ञं योगिनः पर्युपासते।
ब्रह्माग्नावपरे यज्ञं यज्ञेनैवोपजुह्वति॥
transliteration: daivam evāpare yajñaṃ yoginaḥ paryupāsate | brahma-agnāv apare yajñaṃ yajñenaivopajuhvati ||
literal_meaning: Some yogis worship sacrifice directed to the gods; others offer sacrifice into the fire of Brahman itself.
contextual_meaning: Krishna shows Arjuna that people cope with existential anxiety through three main channels: external ritual (pleasing gods), internal ritual (merging into oneness), or relational devotion (offering to a personal God). Each route calms the nervous system, but they differ in how they handle the sense of self.

shadow: Fear of losing self in merger

psychological_state: Decision-paralysis rooted in pluralistic confusion—multiple valid coping strategies create approach-avoidance conflict about which will give lasting safety.
trigger_scenario: A client discovers meditation, yoga, prayer and therapy all claim to reduce anxiety; instead of relief they feel overwhelmed by choice and secretly worry they're 'doing it wrong.'
anchor_text: Calm your fear by offering your life to something greater than yourself.
